evidence_summary: Establish the sudden arrival of a stork to resolve the loss of the scarecrow via mid-air retrieval. The sequence must emphasize the scale difference between the avian savior and the characters on the bank, transitioning from heavy despair to wonder.

After reaching the shore, the group realizes the Scarecrow is gone. A passing Stork arrives and offers assistance. The Stork flies out to the middle of the river, retrieves the Scarecrow from his pole, and delivers him safely back to the group on the bank.
Group stands huddled and defeated on the grassy bank looking at the water.
The group gathers around the safely returned scarecrow on the bank.
Stork arrives, flies to mid-river, grabs the_scarecrow from his pole, and returns him to the bank.
